mission.title: Install and Configure Cadence Workflow on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cadence is a distributed, scalable, durable, and highly available orchestration engine designed to execute asynchronous long-running business logic in a resilient manner. The official Helm chart deploys Cadence backend services (frontend, history, matching, worker) with database support (Cassandra, PostgreSQL, or MySQL).

steps.title: Add the Cadence Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Cadence Helm chart repository.
```bash
helm repo add cadence https://cadence-workflow.github.io/cadence-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cadence using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cadence with default settings (includes Cassandra as the backing store).
```bash
helm install cadence-release cadence/cadence --namespace cadencetest --create-namespace
```
For PostgreSQL instead of Cassandra:
```bash
helm install cadence-release cadence/cadence --namespace cadencetest --create-namespace \
  --set cassandra.enabled=false \
  --set postgresql.enabled=true
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Wait for schema setup jobs to complete, then check that all pods are running.
```bash
kubectl get jobs -n cadencetest
kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cadence-release -n cadencetest
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Customize replica counts and resource limits as needed.
```bash
helm upgrade cadence-release cadence/cadence --namespace cadencetest \
  --set server.frontend.replicaCount=3 \
  --set server.history.replicaCount=3
```
See the chart's values.yaml for all configuration options.

uninstall.title: Remove Cadence deployment
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Cadence Helm release.
```bash
helm delete cadence-release --namespace cadencetest
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cadencetest
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest chart versions.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cadence
upgrade.description:
Upgrade to the latest chart version.
```bash
helm upgrade cadence-release cadence/cadence --namespace cadencetest
```

troubleshooting.title: Schema jobs failing
troubleshooting.description:
Check schema setup job logs if services fail to start.
```bash
kubectl logs job/cadence-release-schema-server -n cadencetest
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure schema jobs completed before services start.
```bash
kubectl get jobs -n cadencetest
kubectl describe pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cadence-release -n cadencetest
```

resolution.summary: Cadence is installed via the official Helm chart from https://cadence-workflow.github.io/cadence-charts. The chart deploys frontend, history, matching, and worker services with automatic schema initialization.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Helm 3.2.0+ installed and PV provisioner support.
